calendar.matters:
APPEAL OF CIVIL JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-16-2020 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Appellant in Pro Per, Ok Soon Key and Counsel for Respondent, Kerry Franich, appeared. Appellant argued, and Counsel for Respondent submitted.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Off Calendar

Motion For Terminating Sanctions As Well As Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 3,010 Against Plaintiff, Including Attorneys Fees And Costs, Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7
Off Calendar Nov-26-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
George S. Wynns, esq. (415-279-7397) for Plaintiff.
Jordan M. Meeks, esq. (415-677-5690) of Severson & Werson for Defendant.

Hearing is held (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT COSTCO WHOLESALE CORPORATION's Motion For Terminating Sanctions As Well As Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 3,010 Against Plaintiff, Including Attorneys Fees And Costs, Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 is DENIED.

Court's order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-28-2019 Continued To Jan-08-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Oct-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted on PLAINTIFF OK KEY's Motion For Reconsideration, as follows:

Plaintiff Ok Soon Key's motion for reconsideration of the Court's August 20, 2019 order on Defendant Costco Wholesale Corporation's demurrer to the first amended complaint is denied. The motion is brought on the false assumption that the Court did not consider Plaintiff's opposition to that demurrer before issuing its ruling. It did. Although the opposition was filed two days late, the Court nevertheless read and considered it before making its ruling. (Had no opposition been filed, the Court would have sustained the demurrer in its entirety without leave to amend.) The motion fails to show any new or different facts, circumstances, or law warranting reconsideration of that order. (Code Civ. Proc. 1008(a).) That Plaintiff was nominally representing herself at the time of the hearing, albeit with the sub rosa assistance and advice of counsel (Wynn Decl. 1; Meeks Decl. 2), is not grounds for special treatment, much less for reconsideration. (See ViaView, Inc. v. Retzlaff (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th198, 208 ["While a party may choose to act as his or her own attorney, '[s]uch a party is to be treated like any other party and is entitled to the same, but no greater consideration than other litigants and attorneys.'"].) Costco's request for sanctions is denied.

Counsel for the defendant is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2019 Continued To Nov-13-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
